step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} Yes — The glucocorticoid receptor (GR) forms a complex with glucocorticoids that translocates to the nucleus and binds to specific DNA elements (e.g., GREs and nGREs) to regulate gene transcription through both transactivation and transrepression mechanisms. It directly interacts with genomic DNA to influence mRNA synthesis, thereby functioning as a transcription factor.
raw_response:
\boxed{A} Yes — The glucocorticoid receptor (GR) forms a complex with glucocorticoids that translocates to the nucleus and binds to specific DNA elements (e.g., GREs and nGREs) to regulate gene transcription through both transactivation and transrepression mechanisms. It directly interacts with genomic DNA to influence mRNA synthesis, thereby functioning as a transcription factor.
\boxed{A}
